name: Breton Apple Pie
recipeInstructions:
1. Butter a 10-inch or 9-inch springform pan. Line the bottom with parchment paper or wax paper cut to fit.
2. Cream the butter until light.
3. Slowly add sugar, beating until the mixture lightens.
4. Beat in rum and vanilla. Add egg yolks one at a time, beating until the mixture is very smooth and light.
5. Beat in flour until absorbed, without overmixing.
6. Place half of the dough in the pan and press with fingertips evenly over the bottom and out 1 inch up the sides.
7. Spread the Apple Filling over the dough.
8. Flour the remaining dough and press into a 10-inch or 9-inch disk on cardboard or a tart pan bottom.
9. Slide the dough over the filling and press into place, making sure the sides are straight and even.
10. Brush the top with egg wash. Trace a lattice pattern on top with fork tines.
11. Bake at 350°F for 40 to 45 minutes, until deep golden and slightly risen. Cool briefly in the pan, then unmold onto a rack to cool. Makes about 8 servings.
Apple Filling:
- 2 pounds Golden Delicious apples
- 1/2 cup Sugar
- 3 tablespoons Butter
- 2 tablespoons Lemon juice
- 2 tablespoons Dark rum
Instructions for Apple Filling:
1. Peel, core, and slice the apples into a large saucepan.
2. Add sugar, butter, lemon juice, and rum to the apples.
3. Cook, covered, until the apples give off juices.
4. Remove the cover and reduce heat. Continue cooking until the juices evaporate.
5. Cool before using.
